data.body:
PLAN OF RECORD (converged design, brainstorm 2026-07-08) — supersedes the staged-build sketch in the earlier DSO-prior-art comment on stage-1 mechanism.

## Where it runs
A store-wide overlap step invoked DURING `review_plan` (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:352), firing before claim — the tolerant, seconds-OK window. It is a STORE-WIDE step (this ticket vs. the store), NOT a per-ticket criterion, which reconciles with this epic's "outside the plan-review gate" scope: same window, wider data scope. It is ADVISORY ONLY — it never blocks the claim.

## Retrieval pipeline (two-stage retrieve-then-rerank; grounded in 2024-2026 IR/dedup research)
- Stage 0 — corpus representation (Cupid, arXiv:2308.10022): each ticket has an LLM-extracted digest {problem_keywords, component/area, key_entities, 2-6 atomic problem/repro propositions}; logs/traces/code discarded. Computed ONCE, async, cached in the SHARED store as a content-hash-keyed record on the ticket (same shape as the kind-keyed `attestations` map, reducer/_present.py:53) so validity is computed on read and content drift invalidates it. Shared = one client's enrichment is a cache-hit for all; no repeated billable calls. NO embeddings, NO vector DB, NO new dependency — reuses the Anthropic LLM rebar already has.
- Stage 1 — candidate generation (cheap, non-LLM): BM25F "More Like This" of the query ticket's digest against the corpus digests (title/problem-statement weighted high; max_doc_freq + stopwords prune boilerplate). Brute-force over a few hundred → sub-10ms, no ANN (unanimous research verdict: ANN only pays off >100K vectors; we are 100-1000x below). Take top ~20. EXCLUDE the query ticket's own graph (parents, children, siblings, and already-linked tickets).
- Stage 2 — precision judge (bounded LLM): pairwise judge over the ~20 candidates — both orderings aggregated (position bias), REQUIRED to cite the shared concrete artifact, DEFAULTS to "related-but-distinct" without one, abstains when unsure. Surfaces a small cap (~3) of candidate duplicates/supersedes/depends_on relations as ADVISORY findings, each with a ready-to-run `rebar link <A> <B> <rel>` command. Never auto-links.

## Query vs corpus (confirmed with product owner)
The 60-min soak governs the search CORPUS, not the query. The ticket completing plan review is NOT yet in the corpus (its own digest is enqueued on its cert, drained ≥60 min later). Its query is built at review time from its CURRENT FULL TEXT — one synchronous extraction piggybacked on the already-assembled plan-review context. Query-side digest vs corpus-side digests = apples-to-apples.

## Async enrichment queue (product-owner spec, realized on rebar's grain; grounded in broker-less-queue prior art)
- Queue = EVENTS in the shared store, keyed by ticket_id → "new certification replaces the old entry" is the natural latest-wins reduce (BullMQ debounce-mode / Huey put_if_empty). No SQLite/Redis/broker — rebar's event store already is a durable, shared, optimistically-concurrent queue substrate; a separate SQLite file could not live on a shared path nor be visible to all clients.
- Enqueue trigger: on plan-review CERTIFICATION, append an enqueue event with not_before = cert_time + 60min; a re-cert bumps not_before forward → the soak, done as a stored timestamp filtered at drain (deliberately NOT an in-process timer — those starve under continuous edits and don't survive short-lived processes).
- Claim: optimistic-append event (one drainer lands, losers retry) — rebar's existing concurrency primitive — plus a lease/reaper for crashes → at-least-once → summarization is idempotent (overwrite-by-content-hash).

## Drain execution — Tier 1 ONLY in v1
Opportunistic detached drain piggybacked on ordinary rebar invocations, git-gc-auto + npm-update-notifier style: cheap "is-drain-needed?" gate that no-ops, DETACH the LLM work to a child that outlives the command (reuse the existing async-push pattern, _store/push.py:108 — Popen(start_new_session=True, DEVNULL)), batch-cap tickets/run, lenient lock (skip if held), env opt-out. Zero client setup, no daemon, no broker, cross-platform.
Tier 2 (native scheduler registration) and Tier 3 (CI-scheduled drain) are SPUN OUT AS IDEAS — prove the system works daemon-free and CI-free first (many clients have no dedicated CI).

## Graceful-skip (no fallback)
If the [agents] extra is absent, plan-review is disabled, or an API key is absent, the whole feature GRACEFULLY SKIPS — no lexical-over-raw fallback. Consequence: a cold store surfaces little until drains have run; acceptable.

## Research grounding (subagent reports, 2026-07-08)
Two-stage retrieve-then-rerank + brute-force <1k + precision-in-stage-2: GitBugs arXiv:2504.09651, ZeroEntropy bi-vs-cross-encoder guides, arXiv:2505.16967 (cascading LLM judge). Cosine uncalibrated/anisotropic → rank only: arXiv:2504.16318, 2601.16907. Never LLM-read the corpus / lost-in-the-middle: Liu 2307.03172, NoLiMa 2502.05167 (effective length ≤~2K tokens). Cupid LLM-as-feature-extractor: 2308.10022. Bounded background work: git gc --auto / git maintenance, npm update-notifier. Broker-less queue: outbox / BullMQ debounce / Huey put_if_empty; event-sourced-append-beats-mutable-file for git stores.
